title: The wreck, fourth time
why.no-label: The fields have text above them, but no labels. It is not the same thing — and the difference is only heard by someone who is not looking at the screen.
why.fake-button: There is something that looks like a button and is not a button. Try reaching it with Tab.
why.wrong-type: One field claims to accept any text at all, while it actually wants one very specific thing.
why.wrong-field: One field is far too small for what it is asking. Like handing someone a business card to write a letter on.
why.no-action: The form collects data and has no idea where to send it.
why.not-closed: Something is open and never closed, or missing its name.
blocks.0.text: The last wreck. The form on the right works — it fills in, it looks fine, nothing crashes.
blocks.1.text: And it is unusable for half the people.
blocks.3.text: Put the mouse away. Really — take your hand off it.
blocks.4.text: Now fill in the form with the keyboard only. Tab for the next field. Enter to submit.
blocks.5.text: How far do you get? And why do you stop?
blocks.7.text: Go through each field and ask: does it know what it wants? Does the person know what to type? Does the computer know who to read it out to?
blocks.8.text: And finally: is there any way at all to send this?
blocks.9.text: The tests are hidden. A direction, not an answer.
blocks.10.text: A form that works only with a mouse is not broken. It is simply not for everyone.
blocks.11.text: All the mistakes come from the last five lessons.
